title: Span Export to Backend
description: Collecting finished spans, sanitizing large payloads, and exporting trace data to the OpenAI tracing API
interaction_flow: Caller → set_tracing_export_api_key(api_key): public helper in tracing/__init__.py
default_exporter → BackendSpanExporter.__init__(): create httpx.Client(timeout=60s, connect=5s) if not exists
set_tracing_export_api_key → BackendSpanExporter.set_api_key(api_key): clear cached 'api_key' property, store _api_key
_run → _export_batches(force=False): triggered when time >= next_export_time OR qsize >= export_trigger_size (70% of max_queue_size=8192)
_export_batches → queue.get_nowait(): drain up to max_batch_size=128 items per batch
BackendSpanExporter.export: group items by item.tracing_api_key (per-item key overrides global)
BackendSpanExporter.export: for each item → item.export() → dict payload
BackendSpanExporter.export → _should_sanitize_for_openai_tracing_api(): True if endpoint == default ingest URL
BackendSpanExporter.export: build payload = {data: [sanitized_dicts]}, headers = {Authorization, Content-Type, OpenAI-Beta}
BackendSpanExporter.export → httpx.Client.post(url, headers, json=payload): HTTP POST
OpenAI Tracing API → httpx.Client: HTTP response
BackendSpanExporter.export: if status<300 → success; if 4xx → log error, no retry; if 5xx → exponential backoff+jitter, max_retries=3
key_relationships: default_exporter() lazily creates BackendSpanExporter (global _global_exporter, protected by _global_lock)
default_processor() lazily creates BatchTraceProcessor wrapping default_exporter() (global _global_processor)
BackendSpanExporter.api_key is cached_property reading _api_key or OPENAI_API_KEY env var; set_api_key() invalidates cache
important_notes: Items grouped by item.tracing_api_key before export; per-item key overrides the global exporter key.
Sanitization only runs when posting to the default OpenAI ingest endpoint (custom endpoints bypass it).
_truncate_span_field_value handles str, dict, list recursively; unserializable values become a {truncated, original_type, preview} dict.
Retry uses exponential backoff with 10% jitter; 4xx errors are NOT retried (client fault).
BackgroundThread is a daemon thread, lazily started on first span/trace enqueue (double-checked locking).
set_api_key() must delete the cached_property from __dict__ before reassigning _api_key.
flush_traces() is the public API for immediate delivery (e.g., end of Celery task or FastAPI background job).
Organization and Project headers (OpenAI-Organization, OpenAI-Project) are added when configured.
